message:
fix(bench): harden concurrent benchmark with proper pragma separation and fail-fast errors

This commit improves the reliability and correctness of the persistent
concurrent write benchmark by addressing several issues discovered during
code review:

**Pragma handling split into setup vs session contexts:**
- `apply_setup_pragmas_fsqlite()`: Runs once during DB creation, includes
  `page_size = 4096` which must be set before any writes occur
- `apply_session_pragmas_fsqlite()`: Runs per-thread, excludes page_size
  since it cannot be changed after the database has been created
- Added `run_fsqlite_pragma()` helper that properly panics on pragma
  failures instead of silently ignoring errors with `let _ =`

**Row ID simplification:**
- Removed `ROW_ID_MULTIPLIER` constant (was 100,000)
- Since each thread writes to its own table (bench_0, bench_1, etc.),
  row IDs can simply be 0..ROWS_PER_THREAD without cross-thread collision
- This makes FrankenSQLite row IDs match C SQLite exactly for fair comparison

**BEGIN CONCURRENT retry hardening:**
- Added retry limit check using the existing MAX_TXN_RETRIES (100) constant
- Added exponential backoff: sleep duration scales with retry count
  (100 * retry_count microseconds) to reduce contention under pressure
- Panic on retry exhaustion instead of infinite loop

**Fail-fast error handling:**
- INSERT retry exhaustion now panics instead of eprintln + break
- Corruption detection now panics instead of eprintln + break
- Rationale: benchmarks must fail loudly on errors to surface bugs;
  silently continuing masks problems and produces misleading results

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
